shower.thought: We spend our whole lives learning to want things, then our whole retirement learning not to.
The people who say "money doesn't matter" usually have enough of it to not think about it.
You've never actually seen the color you're looking at—you've only seen your brain's interpretation of it.
Every photo you take is a moment you decided was worth experiencing through a screen instead of your eyes.
We call it "falling" in love, but we're really just deciding that another person's chaos is worth more than our peace.
The scariest part of getting old isn't losing your body—it's watching everyone else's bodies change and realizing you never really knew what anyone looked like.
You learn the most about someone not from what they do when they're being watched, but from what they do when they could be.
Most of your personality is just your trauma with better branding.
We invented the concept of "guilty pleasure" so we could enjoy things while apologizing for them.
If you've ever felt lonely in a room full of people, you've experienced what your phone feels like all the time.
The people who brag about how little sleep they need are bragging about a symptom of poor health.
You're nostalgic for a version of people that never actually existed—just the version your memory edited for you.
A conversation you regret probably mattered way less to the other person than it does to you.
We're all just pretending we understand how things work well enough to get by.
The hardest part of changing your mind isn't admitting you were wrong—it's forgiving yourself for believing it in the first place.
You can't really know if someone is a good listener or if they're just better at remembering the things you told them.
The moment you become an adult is the moment you stop asking why things are the way they are and start asking how you're going to deal with it.
We keep our phones closer than our partners and call it multitasking.
Your sense of humor is just a catalog of your traumas that you've decided are now funny.
Every time you describe something as "indescribable," you're describing how limited language actually is.

motivational.thought: The quality of your life is determined by the quality of the questions you ask yourself. — Tony Robbins
Comfort is the enemy of growth, but panic is too. Find the edge between them. — Unknown
You are not behind. You are not ahead. You are exactly where your choices have taken you. — Unknown
The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones. — Confucius
Discipline is choosing between what you want now and what you want most. — Unknown
Your life doesn't get better by chance, it gets better by change. — Jim Rohn
The only person you are destined to become is the person you decide to be. — Ralph Waldo Emerson
Stop waiting for the perfect moment. Take the moment and make it perfect. — Unknown
Regret is the tax you pay for inaction. — Unknown
The cost of staying the same will eventually exceed the cost of change. — Unknown
We suffer more in imagination than in reality. — Seneca
You can't heal what you won't acknowledge. — Unknown
Everything hard you do now is invested in the person you're becoming. — Unknown
The people who say it can't be done are usually interrupted by those doing it. — Unknown
Strength doesn't come from what you can do. It comes from overcoming the things you thought you couldn't. — Unknown
Your potential is endless, but your time is finite. Choose accordingly. — Unknown
Envy is the art of counting someone else's blessings instead of your own. — Unknown
The greatest wealth is health. The greatest health is time. Protect both. — Unknown
Progress whispers. Regret screams. Learn to listen to the quieter voice. — Unknown
Obsession with the outcome will kill you. Obsession with the process will set you free. — Unknown

darkjoke.thought:
I told my girlfriend she was drawing her eyebrows too high.
She looked surprised.
I have a stepladder because my real ladder left when I was just a kid.
The worst time to have a heart attack is during a game of charades.

I said to my friend, "You're a real snake."
He said, "Thanks for noticing. I've been working on my hiss."

My dad said, "Son, I'm proud of you."
I said, "For what?"
He said, "I don't know yet. I'm speaking hypothetically."
I'm reading a book on the history of glue. Can't put it down.
My friend has kleptomania. But when you think about it, he's just taking things one step at a time.

I told my wife she was being irrational.
Now I'm sleeping on the couch, which is also irrational, but apparently I'm the one who has to understand that.

My grandfather said, "Your generation is too reliant on technology."
So I unplugged his life support to prove him wrong.

I tried to tell my friend a chemistry joke once.
There was no reaction.

My therapist told me I needed to work on my anger management.
So I did. I killed her.

My son asked me, "Dad, why did you and Mom get divorced?"
I said, "Well, we just grew apart."
He said, "You were married for 23 years."
I said, "Yeah, we grew apart really slowly."

I went to a funeral where they served food.
I told the widow it was the best meal I'd had all year.
She said, "It was yesterday."

My friend said he was feeling suicidal.
I said, "Hey, don't do anything hasty. Call me if you need to talk."
He never called. I guess he really committed to the bit.

I told my dentist my teeth were yellow.
He said, "That's fine, we can fix that."
I said, "No, I mean they're literally the color yellow. I'm a canary."
He said, "That explains why you're so annoyed about the bills."

My mom said, "You need to get out more."
So I did. The cops said I need to stay in more.

I asked my boss for a raise.
He laughed so hard he couldn't hear me ask again.

My friend said, "I'm going through a lot right now."
I said, "Want to talk about it?"
He said, "Not really."
So I didn't listen. Really honored his wishes.

I'm on a seafood diet.
I see food and I remember why I hate myself.

My doctor said I had six months to live.
When I couldn't pay the bill, he said I had a year.
